_comment_top: OAuth2 Client Credentials configuration template for S/4HANA On-Premise.
The S/4HANA On-Premise OAuth2 server is configured via transaction SOAUTH2.
This file documents all required fields; replace all <placeholder> values before use.
Do NOT commit real client_secret values — use environment variables or a secrets vault.

token_endpoint._comment: Token URL for S/4HANA On-Premise OAuth2. Format: https://<sap-host>:<port>/sap/bc/sec/oauth2/token. The path /sap/bc/sec/oauth2/token is fixed — do not change it. Port is typically 443 (HTTPS) or 8443 for non-default HTTPS. ICF node /sap/bc/sec/oauth2 must be active (transaction SICF).

client_id._comment: The OAuth2 client ID registered in SOAUTH2 → OAuth 2.0 Clients → Client ID. In S/4HANA On-Premise, the client ID is the SAP system user name created specifically for OAuth2 (e.g., OAUTH_EXT_CLIENT). The system user must have role SAP_BC_WEBSERVICE_CONSUMER assigned.

client_secret._comment: The client secret set in SOAUTH2 → OAuth 2.0 Clients → Client Secret. Store this in an environment variable (e.g., SAP_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET) — never hard-code in source. The secret is shown only once at creation time in SOAUTH2.

grant_type._comment: For server-to-server integrations use 'client_credentials'. For user-delegated access use 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:saml2-bearer' (SAML Bearer Assertion). Client credentials is the most common pattern for batch integrations and backend system calls.

scope._comment: OAuth2 scope(s) granted to this client, space-separated. In S/4HANA On-Premise, scopes are defined in SOAUTH2 → OAuth 2.0 Scopes and linked to ICF nodes or OData services. Example scope for OData API access: 'ZAPI_PO_READ ZAPI_PO_WRITE'. If the client has only one scope, set it here; if unscoped access is configured, leave empty string.

sap_client._comment: SAP client number (Mandant), passed as x-sap-client HTTP header on every API call. Separate from OAuth2 token request — the token endpoint does not use sap-client, but all OData/RFC calls do. Example: '100' for development, '800' for production.

token_request_headers._comment: Standard HTTP headers for the token POST request. Content-Type must be application/x-www-form-urlencoded. The Authorization header encodes client_id:client_secret as Base64 for HTTP Basic authentication (required by S/4HANA SOAUTH2 token endpoint).

token_request_body_example._comment: URL-encoded form body sent to the token endpoint. grant_type and scope are the required parameters. client_id/client_secret may alternatively be placed here (body method) instead of the Authorization header — SAP SOAUTH2 supports both, but Authorization header is preferred.

token_response_example._comment: SAP SOAUTH2 returns a standard OAuth2 token response. access_token is a Bearer token. expires_in is in seconds (default 3600 = 1 hour on S/4HANA On-Premise). token_type is always 'Bearer'. Cache the token and refresh before expiry rather than requesting a new token on every API call.

api_call_headers._comment: Headers to include on every OData API call after obtaining the token. The Authorization header carries the Bearer token. x-sap-client specifies the SAP client (Mandant). For POST/PATCH/DELETE, also include X-CSRF-Token obtained via a prior GET with X-CSRF-Token: Fetch.

ssl_notes._comment: S/4HANA On-Premise uses a self-signed or private-CA certificate by default. Import the SAP server certificate into your Java truststore (keytool) or Python certifi bundle, or set verify=False temporarily during development (never in production). The SAP certificate can be exported from transaction STRUST → SSL client SSL Client (Standard).

prerequisite_sap_transactions._comment: SAP-side setup required before OAuth2 works. All must be completed by the SAP Basis team before external clients can authenticate.
prerequisite_sap_transactions.SOAUTH2: Create OAuth2 Client, define Scopes, link Scopes to ICF nodes or OData services
prerequisite_sap_transactions.SU01: Create system user (type 'S' - Service) for the OAuth2 client; assign required roles
prerequisite_sap_transactions.SICF: Activate ICF node /sap/bc/sec/oauth2 and the target OData service nodes (e.g., /sap/opu/odata)
prerequisite_sap_transactions.STRUST: Import external CA certificate if the calling system's HTTPS cert is not from a trusted CA
